methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Dragonfly Aerospace is a Series G-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation landscape in Space & Aerospace comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median P/S of 42.9x, and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Axiom Space, though meaningful peer multiples at identical stage and valuation tier remain limited.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method here. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G progression yields $51M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be deployed without disclosed revenue; peer-median triangulation fails due to insufficient narrow-stage datapoints (only 2 same- or adjacent-stage peers identified against a 3-minimum threshold). 

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $59M, centered on $51M. Key uncertainties include the absence of proprietary revenue or margin data, which limits validation via public multiples, and the sparse private peer set at this exact stage. Any investment decision should be heavily conditioned on pipeline visibility, unit economics, and customer concentration metrics not captured in this quantitative framework.
